At the start, following DeepSeek-R1~\citep{guo2025deepseek}, works~\citep{huang2025vision,yang2025r1,shen2025vlm,feng2025video,jiang2025vknowu} enhances MLLMs’ multi-step visual reasoning through reasoning-centric training with RL-style optimization. However, these methods still conduct reasoning primarily in text space, which can be indirect and inefficient as a representation of visual understanding.
In parallel, when from thinking about images to thinking with images, works emphasize tool use during reasoning. Visual Sketchpad~\citep{hu2024visual} generates executable annotation programs to create intermediate visual aids, and DeepEyes~\citep{zheng2025deepeyes} predicts regions and invokes diverse tools for iterative, fine-grained grounding.   
润色

At the outset, following DeepSeek-R1~\citep{guo2025deepseek}, several works~\citep{huang2025vision,yang2025r1,shen2025vlm,feng2025video,jiang2025vknowu} enhance MLLMs’ multi-step visual reasoning via reasoning-centric training with RL-style optimization. However, these methods still perform reasoning primarily in the textual space, which can be an indirect and inefficient proxy for visual understanding.  

In parallel, a line of work shifts from *thinking about images* to *thinking with images* by emphasizing tool use during reasoning. Visual Sketchpad~\citep{hu2024visual} generates executable annotation programs to construct intermediate visual aids, while DeepEyes~\citep{zheng2025deepeyes} predicts regions and invokes diverse tools for iterative, fine-grained grounding.

DistillVLM~\citep{fang2021compressing} implements transformer distillation by aligning hidden attention distributions and feature maps using MSE loss, while MAD~\citep{wang2022multimodal} focuses on aligning visual and text token features between teacher and student via token selection. 重写但不改变愿意

DistillVLM~\citep{fang2021compressing} performs transformer distillation by using an MSE loss to match the teacher and student’s hidden attention distributions and feature maps. In contrast, MAD~\citep{wang2022multimodal} emphasizes aligning visual and textual token features between teacher and student, leveraging token selection to guide the matching.

Furthermore, advanced approaches like LLaVA-MoD~\citep{shu2024llava} minimize the Kullback-Leibler divergence of output distributions and incorporate Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) to refine sample quality discrimination. Beyond logit-level supervision, VIRAL~\citep{yoon2025visual} imposes regularization on intermediate representations to effectively preserve visual semantics. 重写但不改变原意，避免查重

Moreover, more advanced methods such as LLaVA-MoD~\citep{shu2024llava} distill knowledge by reducing the KL divergence between teacher and student output distributions, and further adopt Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) to improve the model’s ability to distinguish higher-quality samples. Moving beyond pure logit supervision, VIRAL~\citep{yoon2025visual} additionally regularizes intermediate features, helping retain visual semantic information more effectively.
